title: herpestis amplexicaulis
intro: Herpestis amplexicaulis, also known as Indian hyssop, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Indian hyssop include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its use as a digestive aid and to treat skin conditions. Indian hyssop can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
